records.ety: [Gr. <?/ containing an integer and one third (i. e., , or in the ratio of 4 to 3); 'epi` upon, over + <?/ the third: cf. L. epitritos, F. pitrite.]

defs: A foot consisting of three long syllables and one short syllable.
notes: ☞ It is so called from being compounded of a spondee (which contains 4 times) with an iambus or a trochee (which contains 3 times). It is called 1st, 2d, 3d, or 4th epitrite according as the short syllable stands 1st, 2d, etc.
